behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the files (rpc_visitor/mod.rs, sui-display/src/v2/value.rs, base_types), the struct 0x1::type_name::TypeName, the field 'name', and the existing layout functions to mirror.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what needs to be recognized/added but doesn't provide the exact code; it just points to the pattern to follow.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact strings like 'use crate::base_types::type_name_layout;', 'use sui_types::base_types::type_name_layout;', and function name 'type_name_layout()' — a correct alternative using a different name (e.g., 'type_name_struct_layout' or glob import) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests include cargo build/test subprocess executions (test_type_name_layout_exists builds an example that calls type_name_layout()) and run cargo test suites, though several tests are grep-based.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_type_name_layout_exists asserts specific struct tag address, module name, field name and count via a compiled example, so a stub returning default layout would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests verify the layout function exists, is imported in both modules, and is used in special-case handling, matching the instruction's required behavior.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are individually named and docstringed with fail-to-pass / pass-to-pass labels.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers appear consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the two files to modify, but localization is arguably part of the task description and not a buggy-line-level hint.
